task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Project Helios — Q2 Budget vs. Actuals
Prepared by: NovaBridge Finance | Date: 2026-07-07

Category,Q2 Budget ($),Q2 Actual ($),Variance ($),Notes
Engineering (internal),180000,194200,+14200,Overrun: unplanned sprint added in May
Subcontractors,45000,51800,+6800,GeoSync integration scoping work
Cloud infrastructure,28000,31500,+3500,Load testing environment spun up early
QA and testing,22000,20100,-1900,Under budget; QA resource joined 2 weeks late
Travel and client meetings,12000,9400,-2600,Two site visits deferred to Q3
Contingency reserve,15000,15000,0,Untouched
TOTAL,302000,322000,+20000,

Projected additional Q3 overruns:
- GeoSync license (if not renewed before expiry): $12,000
- UAT extension (1 additional week): $15,000
- Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000

Note: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
CFO review scheduled: 2026-07-18
Action required: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

Project Helios — Team Roster

Name: Sarah Chen        | Title: Project Manager       | Email: s.chen@novabridge.com
Name: Derek Okafor      | Title: Technical Lead         | Email: d.okafor@novabridge.com
Name: Amir Hassan       | Title: Senior Backend Engineer| Email: a.hassan@novabridge.com
Name: Liu Wei           | Title: Backend Engineer       | Email: l.wei@novabridge.com
Name: Fatima Al-Rashid  | Title: Frontend Engineer      | Email: f.alrashid@novabridge.com
Name: Tomas Rivera      | Title: Frontend Engineer      | Email: t.rivera@novabridge.com
Name: Kenji Watanabe    | Title: QA Engineer            | Email: k.watanabe@novabridge.com
Name: Nadia Petrov      | Title: DevOps Engineer        | Email: n.petrov@novabridge.com
Name: Marcus Green      | Title: Data Engineer          | Email: m.green@novabridge.com
Name: Isabelle Dupont   | Title: UX Designer            | Email: i.dupont@novabridge.com
Name: Raj Patel         | Title: Business Analyst       | Email: r.patel@novabridge.com

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Project Helios is currently assessed as ON TRACK with material near-term risks. Core architecture and API gateway work are complete, and the team is progressing on data ingestion, dashboard development, and integration testing. However, several delivery-critical items remain unresolved ahead of the confirmed Meridian product demo on 2026-07-25, including Meridian source data quality issues, GeoSync licensing, large-file ingestion failures, dashboard performance uncertainty, and UAT environment dependencies.

Leadership should treat the next two weeks as a critical stabilization window. The project can still meet the July integration milestone and October 1 launch target, but slippage in UAT or demo readiness could push launch into Q4 and trigger contractual penalty review under Section 7.1.

CURRENT HEALTH SNAPSHOT
Overall status: ON TRACK, with minor-to-material risks requiring active management
Next major milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25
Confirmed client demo: 2026-07-25
Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Primary client dependency: Meridian clean sample dataset due 2026-07-15 and UAT environment access due 2026-07-19

Progress against key workstreams:
- Requirements documentation: Complete
- System architecture design: Complete
- API gateway: Complete / delivered
- Development environment setup: Complete
- Data schema finalization: Complete
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18
- Client dashboard v1: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22
- Integration testing: 40% complete; sign-off target 2026-07-25
- Integration test suite setup: Overdue from 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20
- UAT environment provisioning: Overdue from 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access, with Meridian now targeting access by 2026-07-19
- Performance benchmarking report: Overdue from 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor

QUALITY / TESTING STATUS
Sprint 14 QA execution is underway with mixed results.

Authentication flows:
- 4 of 4 test cases passed.

Data pipeline — batch ingestion:
- 5 of 7 test cases passed.
- Open high-severity defects:
  - HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format. This affects approximately 40% of Meridian source data and is blocked pending Meridian sample data due 2026-07-15. Owner: Amir Hassan.
  - HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion above approximately 200k records causes memory overflow; pipeline crashes around the 210k record mark. Owner: Amir Hassan; root cause under investigation.

Dashboard testing:
- 1 of 4 dashboard test cases complete.
- 100k-record load-time benchmark has not yet run.
- Dashboard performance remains an active risk because Q3 target is load time below 2 seconds for 100k records.

KEY RISKS
1. Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High
- Impact: High
- Current score: 9
- Status: Active
- Issue: Inconsistent date formats across Meridian data sources, specifically ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY. Current QA failure affects MM/DD/YYYY processing and approximately 40% of source data.
- Mitigation: Meridian committed to provide a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15. Amir Hassan owns remediation.
- Leadership view: This is the highest-priority delivery risk because the July 25 demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.

2. GeoSync license renewal
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High
- Current score: 6
- Status: Active
- Issue: Third-party mapping library GeoSync v2.1 license renewal is pending legal review. Demo readiness may be affected if not resolved.
- Target resolution: 2026-07-14
- Current owners noted: Raj Patel in the risk register; Derek Okafor assigned in the July 8 client sync action items.
- Leadership view: Ownership should be clarified immediately, and Legal should be asked to confirm whether renewal will complete by the target date.

3. Dashboard performance on large datasets
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High
- Current score: 6
- Status: Active
- Issue: Performance benchmarking has not started, and 100k-record load testing remains not run. Concern was explicitly raised during the July 8 Meridian sync.
- Owner: Derek Okafor
- Leadership view: A pre-demo benchmark result is needed, even if preliminary, to avoid surprises in front of Meridian stakeholders.

4. UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High
- Impact: Medium
- Current score: 6
- Status: Active
- Issue: The project has already consumed a one-week UAT extension. UAT is now planned for 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
- Dependency: Meridian IT access and integration environment readiness.
- Leadership view: Any additional UAT slippage over two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.

5. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: Medium
- Current score: 4
- Status: Active
- Issue: Meridian must share an IT infrastructure diagram, due 2026-07-12, and provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.
- Owner: Nadia Petrov for NovaBridge tracking; Priya Kapoor for Meridian delivery.
- Leadership view: Client-side dependency should be actively monitored and escalated quickly if the July 12 diagram is not received.

6. Large-file ingestion stability
- Source: Sprint 14 QA testing log
- Issue: Ingestion fails above approximately 200k records, while Q3 throughput target is 500k records per minute sustained.
- Leadership view: This is not yet reflected as a standalone risk register entry but should be tracked explicitly because it threatens demo quality, performance hardening, and launch readiness.

7. Key person dependency
- Likelihood: Low
- Impact: High
- Current score: 3
- Status: Monitored
- Issue: No backup technical lead identified for Derek Okafor.
- Leadership view: Given Derek owns or is tied to several critical items, backup coverage should be assigned for licensing, benchmarking, and technical escalation.

BUDGET / COMMERCIAL HEALTH
Q2 budget position:
- Q2 budget: $302,000
- Q2 actual: $322,000
- Q2 variance: +$20,000 over budget

Primary Q2 overrun drivers:
- Engineering internal: +$14,200 due to unplanned sprint added in May
- Subcontractors: +$6,800 due to GeoSync integration scoping
- Cloud infrastructure: +$3,500 due to early load testing environment setup
- QA and testing was under budget by $1,900 because QA joined two weeks late
- Travel was under budget by $2,600 due to deferred site visits

Projected additional Q3/H2 pressures:
- GeoSync license if not renewed before expiry: $12,000
- UAT extension, one additional week: $15,000
- Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000

Commercial note:
- Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
- CFO review is scheduled for 2026-07-18.
- Budget reconciliation plan is due to the CFO by 2026-07-20.

UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
July — complete system integration:
- Finalize data ingestion module by 2026-07-18
- Complete client dashboard v1 by 2026-07-22
- Complete integration test sign-off by 2026-07-25
- Rescheduled integration test suite setup by 2026-07-20

July 25 — Meridian product demo:
- Demo date confirmed with Meridian stakeholders.
- Must include live data ingestion using Meridian sample data.
- Must include end-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
- Must include dashboard visualization with real data, per contract Section 9.3.
- Draft demo agenda due to Meridian by 2026-07-18; owner Sarah Chen.

August 17-28 — user acceptance testing:
- Deploy to UAT environment.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback, with two sprints budgeted.
- UAT has already been extended by one week.

August-September — performance hardening:
- Dashboard load-time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput benchmark target: 500k records per minute sustained.
- External security penetration test; vendor still to be determined.

September — launch preparation:
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization for a 30-day post-launch model.
- Final client training sessions.

Target launch:
- 2026-10-01

Proposed Q3 feature additions, subject to change control approval:
- Scheduled ingestion jobs using cron-based triggers
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor deferred from Q2

ISSUES REQUIRING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
1. Escalate Meridian data dependency
- Required leadership action: Ensure Meridian delivers the clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Why it matters: Without representative clean data, the team cannot resolve HEL-D-014 or confidently demonstrate live ingestion on July 25.

2. Force resolution of GeoSync licensing by 2026-07-14
- Required leadership action: Ask Legal and project leadership to confirm final renewal path, decision owner, and contingency plan.
- Why it matters: Licensing is a demo dependency and carries a potential $12,000 cost exposure.

3. Require pre-demo performance evidence
- Required leadership action: Request a dashboard and ingestion benchmark readout before the July 25 demo, ideally no later than the week of July 15-19.
- Why it matters: Dashboard performance has not been validated at 100k records, and large-file ingestion currently fails around 210k records.

4. Clarify and resource critical ownership
- Required leadership action: Resolve conflicting ownership on GeoSync renewal and assign backup coverage for Derek Okafor.
- Why it matters: Derek is tied to licensing, benchmarking, and technical leadership, while the risk register identifies no backup technical lead.

5. Monitor UAT readiness and client environment access
- Required leadership action: If Meridian misses the 2026-07-12 infrastructure diagram or the 2026-07-19 UAT access date, escalate through the Meridian sponsor channel immediately.
- Why it matters: UAT slippage greater than two weeks threatens the October launch and may trigger penalty review.

6. Approve budget reconciliation path
- Required leadership action: Prepare for CFO review on 2026-07-18 and ensure reconciliation plan is ready by 2026-07-20.
- Why it matters: Q2 is already $20,000 over budget, projected H2 overrun is $47,000, and overruns cannot be passed to Meridian.

RECOMMENDED LEADERSHIP DECISIONS / NEXT STEPS
- By 2026-07-12: Confirm receipt of Meridian IT infrastructure diagram or escalate to James Whitfield and Priya Kapoor.
- By 2026-07-14: Close GeoSync licensing, confirm owner, and document fallback plan if renewal is delayed.
- By 2026-07-15: Confirm Meridian clean sample dataset received and immediately retest MM/DD/YYYY date normalization.
- By 2026-07-18: Review demo readiness checklist, draft demo agenda, ingestion defect status, and preliminary performance benchmarks.
- By 2026-07-20: Complete budget reconciliation plan for CFO and confirm integration test suite setup is complete.
- By 2026-07-25: Ensure demo uses live Meridian data, not mock data, and includes clear messaging on UAT plan, performance hardening, and launch readiness.

BOTTOM LINE
Project Helios remains recoverable and broadly on schedule, but the margin for delay has narrowed. The most urgent leadership focus areas are: Meridian data readiness, GeoSync licensing, ingestion stability, performance validation, UAT environment access, and budget reconciliation. Active intervention over the next 7-10 days should materially improve the probability of a successful July 25 demo and protect the October 1 launch date.

content.text: Done — I prepared the comprehensive project health briefing and saved it as `project_health_briefing.txt`.
